label: OpenTelemetry Collector
description: Send traces, metrics, and logs to any OpenTelemetry Collector via OTLP gRPC or HTTP.

form.title: Connect OpenTelemetry Collector
form.subtitle: Configure an OTLP endpoint so Anton can export traces, metrics, and logs to your collector.

methods.label: OTLP / HTTP
methods.description: Export telemetry over OTLP HTTP (port 4318). Paste your collector URL and an optional bearer token or custom headers. Works with any OTLP-compatible backend.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OTLP / HTTP

OTLP HTTP is the simplest export path — no gRPC library required, works through most proxies and firewalls.

### Steps

1. Find your collector's OTLP HTTP address. The default port is **4318**. Example: `http://localhost:4318` or `https://ingest.example.com:4318`.
2. If your collector or SaaS backend requires authentication, locate the bearer token or API key in your backend's dashboard (e.g. Grafana Cloud → My Account → API Keys, Honeycomb → Team Settings → API Keys).
3. Paste the base URL (without `/v1/traces`) into **Endpoint URL** below.
4. If a bearer token is required, paste it into **Bearer token**. Anton will add `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to every request.
5. For non-standard auth (e.g. `x-honeycomb-team` headers), add them as a JSON object in **Extra headers**.
6. Leave **Insecure (skip TLS)** checked only for localhost development collectors.

### Notes

- Anton appends the signal paths automatically (`/v1/traces`, `/v1/metrics`, `/v1/logs`).
- If your collector runs behind a reverse proxy with a self-signed cert, either install the cert or use the gRPC method with `insecure` mode.
- For managed backends (Honeycomb, Lightstep, Grafana Cloud, etc.) the endpoint and token are shown in their ingestion settings page.

fields.name: endpoint_url
fields.label: Endpoint URL
fields.description: Base URL of your OTLP HTTP receiver. Do not include /v1/traces — Anton appends signal paths automatically.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer token
fields.description: If your collector requires authentication, paste the bearer token here. Anton sends Authorization: Bearer <token> with every request.

fields.name: extra_headers
fields.label: Extra headers
fields.description: Optional JSON object of additional HTTP headers to include on every OTLP request (e.g. vendor-specific auth headers).

fields.name: insecure
fields.label: Insecure (skip TLS verification)
fields.description: Disable TLS certificate verification. Use only for local development collectors — never in production.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout (seconds)
fields.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the collector to acknowledge an export request before timing out.

methods.label: OTLP / gRPC
methods.description: Export telemetry over OTLP gRPC (port 4317). Higher throughput than HTTP; preferred for high-volume or low-latency pipelines.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via OTLP / gRPC

OTLP gRPC uses port 4317 by default and streams telemetry with lower overhead than HTTP. Choose this when throughput matters or your collector is co-located.

### Steps

1. Find your collector's gRPC address. Default port is **4317**. Format: `host:4317` (no `http://` or `https://` prefix for gRPC).
2. If authentication is required, get your bearer token or API key from your backend's settings.
3. Paste the endpoint into **gRPC endpoint** below (e.g. `collector.internal:4317` or `ingest.example.com:4317`).
4. If a bearer token is needed, paste it into **Bearer token**.
5. For additional metadata headers (e.g. `x-honeycomb-team`), add them as a JSON object in **Extra headers (metadata)**.
6. Check **Insecure mode** only for plaintext gRPC on localhost — production collectors should use TLS.

### Notes

- Unlike HTTP, the gRPC endpoint should NOT include a scheme prefix (`http://` / `https://`). TLS is negotiated via the insecure flag.
- Some managed SaaS backends (e.g. Grafana Cloud) expose gRPC at a different hostname than their HTTP endpoint — check their docs.
- If your collector is behind a load balancer, ensure it supports HTTP/2 end-to-end for gRPC to work correctly.

fields.name: grpc_endpoint
fields.label: gRPC endpoint
fields.description: Host and port of your OTLP gRPC receiver. Do not include http:// or https:// — TLS is controlled by the insecure flag below.

fields.name: bearer_token
fields.label: Bearer token
fields.description: If your collector requires authentication, paste the bearer token here. Sent as gRPC metadata: authorization: Bearer <token>.

fields.name: extra_headers
fields.label: Extra headers (metadata)
fields.description: Optional JSON object of additional gRPC metadata key-value pairs to attach to every export request.

fields.name: insecure
fields.label: Insecure mode (plaintext gRPC, no TLS)
fields.description: Send gRPC traffic without TLS. Enable only for local collectors — never expose plaintext gRPC over a public network.

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.label: Timeout (seconds)
fields.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the collector to acknowledge an export RPC before timing out.
